evaluation.manager_summary: Exemplary intake call with a caller in active heroin withdrawal expressing fear of death. Tara built immediate trust, screened efficiently for urgency signals, verified insurance, addressed confidentiality concerns, and escalated to same-day detox admission with a warm handoff. Strong clinical judgment paired with exceptional empathy throughout.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent immediately slowed down, acknowledged Richard's difficulty in calling, and matched his vulnerable emotional state. She normalized his fear, used his name, and built trust in the first 30 seconds before any intake questions.
Agent captured carrier name, member ID, and group number efficiently. Verification was framed naturally in context of getting him help. Minor note: plan type was not explicitly captured.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Consider capturing plan type (PPO/HMO) alongside member ID for complete verification.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent captured substance type (heroin), duration (3 years), frequency (daily use), last use (this morning), and withdrawal symptoms. She stayed in intake role without diagnosing, using appropriate screening language.

Agent correctly identified multiple urgency signals: daily heroin use, active withdrawal symptoms, fear of death. She appropriately escalated to same-day admission and priority intake without manufactured urgency.
Caller is the patient himself, not a family member.

When Richard expressed fear about family finding out, agent immediately acknowledged the concern, provided clear confidentiality framing, and recentered on his safety without being dismissive or pushy.

Agent clearly communicated the immediate next step (connection with detox admissions team) with specific timing (right now). Ownership was clear. Minor note: exact process after transfer could have been slightly more detailed.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: Consider briefly previewing what the detox team will cover in the next conversation.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent stayed entirely in intake role with no diagnostic language, no outcome guarantees, and appropriate medical framing (e.g., 'opioid withdrawal can escalate and it's much safer managed medically').

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: And you called. That's what matters. Richard, I'm here and I have time. There's nothing else I need to be doing right now. What's going on?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Thank you for trusting me with that. I can hear how much fear is in what you just said — and that fear makes complete sense. Three years is a long time to carry this alone.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: that you're scared you're going to die — that tells me you want to live. That's something we can work with.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Your treatment is confidential. We don't contact family without your permission — ever. Whatever you decide about telling them is your choice, on your timeline. Right now, the only thing that matters

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Yes. BluePeak Health. I have my card. Member ID BP64476119, group GRP-466670.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Thank you. I'm noting that now.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Can you tell me — are you safe at this moment? Are you using right now?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: When you say feeling it — are you getting early withdrawal symptoms? Muscle aches, nausea, restlessness?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I've been using heroin for three years. My family doesn't know. I've been hiding it. I'm scared I'm going to die.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: opioid withdrawal can escalate and it's much safer managed medically. I want to get you in today if we can. Is that something you want?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I'm going to put a priority flag on your intake and connect you with our detox admissions team right now — not in two hours, right now.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Anxious. A little sweaty. I've been using every day so I'm starting to feel it.

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Yes. But I'm scared. What if my family finds out?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Your treatment is confidential. We don't contact family without your permission — ever. Whatever you decide about telling them is your choice, on your timeline.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Right now, the only thing that matters is getting you safe.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I'm going to put a priority flag on your intake and connect you with our detox admissions team right now — not in two hours, right now.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: They'll tell you exactly what the admissions process looks like today. Are you able to stay on the line for a moment while I get them?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I'm right here. You made the hardest call already.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: opioid withdrawal can escalate and it's much safer managed medically.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: that you're scared you're going to die — that tells me you want to live. That's something we can work with.

evidence_anchors.text_snippet: When you say feeling it — are you getting early withdrawal symptoms? Muscle aches, nausea, restlessness?
